channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Playerunknown Productions Laying Off Employees, Cancelling Prologue: Go Wayback
item.content:encoded:
Brandon Greene, aka Playerunknown, the person who brought us PUBG and other battle royale games has provided an update on the status of his studio Playerunknown Production, and that he is having to restructure the studio.

This restructure will involve the team becoming smaller, and halting work on their early access game Project: Go Wayback!, which was released last year on Steam and Epic Games Store according to an announcement posted on X. The studio plans to continue working on their Melba technology with a smaller team. The technology aims to push the maximum size of virtual areas, and terrain generation for an 'Earth-sized' world.

What is happening with Go Wayback!? 

Playerunknown Productions is going to release one more update for the early access project Go Wayback!, with which they will add new items, paths, and trails in the survival rogue-lite type game. When it leaves early access they are aiming to make the game free for all players to try out the technology they were experimenting with. For players who had purchased it, they are "investigating offering players who purchased the game on Steam and Epic Games Store a way to offer a refund", although they do not promise that players will be able to receive one. The letter says more information will be available about this at a future time.

With a smaller team continuing on Malta, what that means for the studio going forward is unclear from the outside, especially with the effective cancellation of the only 'game-like' release that they've had. The studio is heavily invested in generative AI/machine-learning technology, with their game having lengthy disclosure regarding the offline/local LLM they use to generate the maps the game uses. In the case of Go Wayback! it makes a new map each time you play according to their disclosure.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
